seoTitle: How to Add a Calendar in Microsoft Loop (2026)
metaDescription: Turn a Microsoft Loop task list into a calendar view or link Outlook events so project timelines stay visible and synced for your team.
intro: Tracking deadlines in a separate app or as a plain bullet list makes it easy to lose sight of important dates. This guide shows how to switch a Loop table into a calendar layout and how to connect an Outlook calendar event so your team's schedule stays visible in one place.

steps.heading: Deploy a tracking matrix
steps.body: Move your cursor to a free line in your Loop page and type /date or /task list to drop a dynamic tracking block onto the canvas.

steps.heading: Switch to calendar view
steps.body: Click the layout switching icon on the table header and change the display to Calendar view.

steps.heading: Populate your milestones
steps.body: Open the date picker to add your specific project milestones and assign ownership to each one.

steps.heading: Link an Outlook calendar event
steps.body: Open your connected calendar event on office.com and choose Insert Loop Component to bring the schedule into your page.

steps.heading: Confirm the sync
steps.body: Review your rendered layout and let Loop auto-sync the calendar components across all active channels.

faq.question: Can I switch back from calendar view to a table?
faq.answer: Yes, the layout switching icon lets you toggle a Loop data grid between table, board, and calendar views at any time.

faq.question: Do calendar updates in Loop reflect in Outlook automatically?
faq.answer: Linking to an Outlook calendar event brings shared scheduling data into your Loop page, though updates should still be verified across both apps.

faq.question: Can multiple people update the calendar view at once?
faq.answer: Yes, since it's built on the same live multiplayer data grid, calendar changes sync in real time for everyone viewing the page.

faq.question: What kind of data appears in the calendar view?
faq.answer: It reflects the date fields from your underlying table or task list, so any milestone with a date assigned will show up on the calendar.
